tickerText: [TENNIS] Queen's Day 3: British wild card Arthur Fery reaches his first ATP quarter-final with a 7-6(7), 6-4 win over Adrian Mannarino on Andy Murray Arena; top seed Alex de Minaur beats Denis Shapovalov 6-4, 6-1 for a third Queen's quarter-final; Brandon Nakashima rolls past Ignacio Buse 6-2, 6-2 — Fery faces Francisco Cerundolo in Friday's British QF clash • [BADMINTON] Macau Open round-of-16 day at the East Asian Games Dome: fourth seed Han Yue rallies past Lee So Yeon 16-21, 22-20, 21-17; Cho Jeong Min/E Na open mixed doubles with a 21-8, 21-11 win — Super 300 field narrows at the $370,000 HSBC BWF World Tour stop • [SQUASH] PSA Tour Finals Day 2 in Paris: Sivasangari tops Watanabe 11-9, 11-6, 11-9 to set a semi-final with world champion Orfi (d. Gilis 11-1, 11-8, 6-11, 11-5); men's slate features Paul Coll vs Joel Makin and Karim Gawad vs Mohamad Zakaria tonight on the all-glass court at Centquatre • [PICKLEBALL] Beijing Open Day 2 at China's National Tennis Center — round of 32 and round of 16 across all events with 384 players chasing $70,000 in pro prize money and 500 PPA Tour Asia points; Championship Sunday set for June 21 at the Olympic venue • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana enters Day 3 (June 16–21); Kanak Jha continues his run at world No. 24 after Skopje silver; United States Smash tickets on sale for the Ontario Convention Center (June 26–July 5)

newsItems.title: Fery's Queen's Breakthrough Caps Historic Day
newsItems.excerpt: British wild card Arthur Fery became the seventh home wild card to reach the Queen's men's quarter-finals, battling past Adrian Mannarino 7-6 (7), 6-4 on Andy Murray Arena despite a mid-match nosebleed. Top seed Alex de Minaur extended his perfect record over Denis Shapovalov with a 6-4, 6-1 win, while Brandon Nakashima joined them in the last eight with a 6-2, 6-2 victory over Ignacio Buse.

newsItems.title: Sivasangari Books Paris Semi Against Orfi
newsItems.excerpt: Malaysia's S. Sivasangari stormed into the PSA Squash Tour Finals semi-finals at Centquatre-Paris with an 11-9, 11-6, 11-9 win over Japan's Satomi Watanabe. Reigning world champion Amina Orfi is waiting after defeating Tinne Gilis 11-1, 11-8, 6-11, 11-5, as the season-ending $710,000 event moves into Day 2 knockout action on June 18.

newsItems.title: Macau Open Reaches Round of 16
newsItems.excerpt: The Sands China Ltd. Macau Open Super 300 shifted into the last 16 on June 18 at the East Asian Games Dome, with early results including a three-game comeback from fourth seed Han Yue over Lee So Yeon (16-21, 22-20, 21-17) and a straight-sets mixed doubles win for Cho Jeong Min and E Na. The $370,000 BWF World Tour stop runs through June 21.

newsItems.title: Beijing Open Hits Main-Draw R16
newsItems.excerpt: Day 2 of the Capital Securities Beijing Open brought round-of-32 and round-of-16 action across all events at China's National Tennis Center — the 2008 Olympic venue hosting PPA Tour Asia for the first time. A 384-player field is competing for $70,000 in pro prize money and 500 tour points, with Championship Sunday set for June 21.
